other_observers: Brian Gibbons, Andy Boyce, Nathan Pieplow, Joey Kellner, Glenn Walbeck, Norm Erthal, Mark Peterson, Lisa Edwards, and six other birders.; Larry Semo originally found the bird. Brandon Percival, Bill Maynard and Joe Roller saw the bird on the 20th.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, in an area that has a lot of willows a little ways east of the Banding Station.
description: I saw it for a short period of time. It had a long tail and long wings. It had a lot of white on the outer tail. Dark upper body and rufous wings.Behaviors: It was calling from a tree and then when we saw it, it flew towards the west and landed in a tree not far from the one it flew from.Call: It gave a repeated whistled whip-poor-will.Plumage: Adult
